name: Mexico

base.summary: Indian passport alone: Mexican visitor visa required before travel.

us_visa.summary: With a valid US (or Canada/Japan/UK/Schengen) visa or residence permit, no separate Mexican visa is needed — stays under 180 days for tourism/business/transit. You get an FMM arrival form; keep it until departure.

green_card.summary: US permanent residents do not need a Mexican visa for short visits.

apply_how: If relying on your US visa: no application — carry passport + valid US visa, complete the arrival/FMM form.
Air entry is the smoother, better-documented path; land-border entries have seen inconsistent enforcement — keep every stamp and form.
Without a US visa: apply at a Mexican consulate with jurisdiction over your residence.
processing_note: No processing when using the US-visa exemption.

sources.title: Embassy of India, Mexico City — notice to Indian nationals travelling to Mexico

name: Canada

base.summary: Visitor visa (TRV) required for Indian citizens — a US visa or H-1B changes nothing.

us_visa.summary: Still a full TRV. Being in the US only changes where you give biometrics (US VACs) — not the requirement.

green_card.summary: CORRECTED: US lawful permanent residents are visa- AND eTA-exempt (since Apr 2022, all travel modes). Carry your valid passport + physical green card. No TRV, no eTA.

apply_how: Apply online through IRCC (ircc.canada.ca) from within the US.
Give biometrics at a US Visa Application Centre (VAC) — book early, slots are scarce in some cities.
Processing times swing wildly — check IRCC's live processing-time tool, do not trust stored numbers.
processing_note: Volatile — always check IRCC's current processing times page.

sources.title: IRCC — US lawful permanent residents (eTA/visa exemption)

name: United Kingdom

base.summary: Standard Visitor visa required for Indian citizens for any visit.

us_visa.summary: Visits still need a visa. TRANSIT: airside transit (no border crossing, same calendar day) is DATV-exempt for Indians holding a valid US, Canada, Australia or NZ visa — verified in Home Office transit guidance. Landside transit (bags/terminal change) has separate, stricter rules — check the official guidance for your exact case; other countries' e-visas don't count.

apply_how: Visit: apply online at gov.uk, biometrics at a VFS centre in the US.
Airside transit with a valid US visa: no DATV needed (arrive and leave airside, onward same day).

Landside transit (<48h, crossing the border): different rules — a Visitor in Transit visa or the transit-without-visa concession conditions; verify your exact routing in the Home Office Transit guidance before booking.
The gov.uk 'Check if you need a UK visa' tool with your exact itinerary is authoritative.
processing_note: Standard Visitor visas from the US commonly take ~3 weeks; varies.

sources.title: Home Office — Transit guidance (official PDF, Mar 2026)
gov.uk — Check if you need a UK visa

notes: Airside branch verified; landside branch conditions are in the Transit guidance PDF — treat landside as verify-first.

name: Schengen Area

base.summary: Schengen short-stay visa required. Your H-1B/US status changes nothing about the requirement.

us_visa.summary: Still required. What your US residence changes: you apply from the US, at the consulate/VFS with jurisdiction over your state, with US immigration documents in your file.

green_card.summary: Still required for Indian passport holders.

apply_how: Apply to the country of main destination (longest stay), at the consulate/VFS with jurisdiction over your US state.
Jurisdiction examples (verify each before booking): Arkansas → most consulates route via Houston (e.g. France-VFS Houston, Germany Houston); some route via Chicago or DC depending on country.
Book appointments 4–8+ weeks ahead in peak season; the appointment shortage is the real constraint, not the paperwork.
You must hold a US visa/status valid well beyond your return date — many consulates require 3+ months validity back into the US.
processing_note: 15 calendar days is the official norm; appointment lead time is the real bottleneck.

sources.title: European Commission — Schengen visa policy (Reg. 2018/1806, India on Annex I)

notes: Visa requirement verified at EU level. The state→consular-jurisdiction stub (Arkansas→Houston examples) remains per-country: confirm jurisdiction on the specific country's consulate page before booking.

name: UAE (Dubai, Abu Dhabi)

base.summary: Indian passport alone: pre-arranged visa (typically via airline/hotel/agent) required.

us_visa.summary: With a valid US visa or green card (each valid ≥ 6 months, passport too), Indian citizens get a 14-day visa on arrival, extendable once. AED 100 at the counter (extension AED 250).

green_card.summary: Green card holders: same 14-day VOA; GC must be valid ≥ 6 months.

apply_how: No pre-application — present passport + valid US visa/GC at the visa-on-arrival counter.
AED 100 fee at the counter; one extension possible (AED 250).
If your supporting document has under 6 months validity, this route is closed — arrange a pre-approved visa instead.
processing_note: On arrival; allow extra time at immigration.

sources.title: u.ae — visa on arrival (official UAE portal)
UAE Cabinet — VOA for Indians with US visa/green card

name: Türkiye

base.summary: Indian passport alone: sticker visa via consulate required.

us_visa.summary: With a valid US/UK/Ireland/Schengen visa or residence permit, Indians can get a single-entry e-Visa online. The supporting document must be valid ON your Türkiye entry date; other countries' e-Visas do NOT count as supporting documents (physical visa/residence permit only).

green_card.summary: Valid US green card also qualifies for the conditional e-Visa.

apply_how: Apply only at the official portal: evisa.gov.tr (beware lookalike sites).
Enter supporting-document (US visa/GC) details exactly; arrival must be by an approved carrier for the conditional e-Visa.
Print the e-Visa; immigration may ask to see the supporting US document.
processing_note: e-Visa is typically issued in minutes.

name: Georgia

base.summary: Indian passport alone: apply via Georgia's e-Visa portal.

us_visa.summary: Holders of valid visas or residence permits of listed countries (incl. the US) may enter visa-free and stay 90 days in any 180-day period (Government Ordinance list).

green_card.summary: Valid US residence also qualifies for the 90/180 visa-free entry.

apply_how: Visa-free path: carry passport + the valid US visa/GC used to qualify.
Otherwise: evisa.gov.ge.

sources.title: geoconsul.gov.ge — entering Georgia (visa-free with listed visas/permits)

name: Armenia

base.summary: CORRECTED: no visa on arrival for Indian citizens — e-Visa in advance only (AMD 3,000/21 days or AMD 15,000/120 days, ~3 business days). Since Nov 2024 Indians additionally need travel insurance plus either a qualifying visa/residence permit or return ticket + invitation + proof of funds.

apply_how: Apply at evisa.mfa.am only (official MFA portal).
Attach mandatory travel insurance (Indian nationals).
Attach a qualifying visa/residence (e.g. valid US visa/GC) OR return ticket + invitation + funds proof.
A temporary visa-free window for Indians with US/EU/GCC residence permits expired 1 July 2026 — do not rely on it.

name: Albania

base.summary: Indian passport alone: visa required.

us_visa.summary: Visa-free up to 90 days in 180 with a valid MULTIPLE-ENTRY US (or UK/Schengen) visa that has been USED at least once. Carry proof of prior US entry (stamps).

green_card.summary: Valid US permanent residence also qualifies for visa-free entry.

apply_how: Visa-free path: passport + valid, previously-used US multiple-entry visa or GC.
Otherwise: e-visa at e-visa.al.

sources.title: Albania MFA — visa regime for foreign citizens

name: Serbia

base.summary: Indian passport alone: visa required (the old visa-free arrangement for Indians was removed).

us_visa.summary: Visa-free up to 90 days in any 6 months with a valid US (or UK/Schengen) visa or residence permit — it must remain valid for your whole stay.

green_card.summary: Valid US residence: same 90-day visa-free entry.

apply_how: Carry passport + the valid US visa/GC.
Otherwise apply at the Serbian embassy with jurisdiction.

name: Japan

base.summary: Visa required for Indian citizens; an eVisa exists for tourism for residents of some countries (incl. Indians resident in the US — verify current scope).

us_visa.summary: Foreign nationals RESIDING in the US (incl. Indians) can use the JAPAN eVISA channel: single-entry tourism up to 90 days. Residence in the US is what qualifies you, not the visa itself.

apply_how: Apply via the official JAPAN eVISA system (mofa.go.jp links to it) as a US resident.
Note: application channels differ by nationality — some nationalities route via MOFA-accredited agencies; confirm your channel on MOFA before paying anyone.
Not eligible / not in the US: paper application via the Japanese consulate with jurisdiction over your state.

name: Singapore

base.summary: Visa required for Indian citizens — apply through an authorized agent/airline; there is no e-visa direct-to-applicant channel.

us_visa.summary: Visits still need a visa. TRANSIT: the 96-hour Visa-Free Transit Facility applies to Indians holding a visa/long-term pass (valid ≥ 1 month from Singapore entry) issued by the US, UK, Canada, Australia, Germany, Japan, NZ or Switzerland — enter by any mode, DEPART BY AIR OR SEA ONLY, onward ticket within 96h, no extensions, entry at ICA officer's discretion.

apply_how: Visit visa: through a Singapore-registered contact/agent or airline (SIA etc.), online via authorized channels.
VFTF transit: no pre-application, but conditions are strict (onward ticket, qualifying visa validity, one-direction use per trip) — check ICA's current VFTF page.

name: Thailand

base.summary: Right now: 60-day visa-free entry for Indians is in force. BUT a May-2026 cabinet decision approved scrapping it — once published in the Royal Gazette, India drops to a 15-day visa on arrival. Check on your TRAVEL date, not your booking date.

apply_how: Visa-free: passport ≥ 6 months validity, onward ticket, funds proof possible.
If the visa-free window lapses: Thailand eVisa portal.
processing_note: Change approved but not yet gazetted as of 2026-07-06 — this record will flip; re-verify at mfa.go.th before every trip.

sources.title: Thai MFA — visa exemption list (official PDF)

name: Vietnam

base.summary: Indian citizens: Vietnam e-Visa up to 90 days, single ($25) or multiple entry ($50), typically 3–7 working days.

apply_how: Apply only at the official portal (evisa.gov.vn); typical processing 3–5 working days.
Print the e-Visa approval; enter via the port you selected in the application.

name: Indonesia (Bali)

base.summary: Indian citizens: Visa on Arrival or e-VOA in advance — 30 days, extendable once (max 60). Passport ≥ 6 months + return ticket.

apply_how: e-VOA at evisa.imigrasi.go.id before flying (official portal; smoother than the airport queue), or pay at the VOA counter on arrival.
Passport ≥ 6 months validity; onward/return ticket required.

sources.title: Direktorat Jenderal Imigrasi — official eVisa portal

name: Malaysia

base.summary: Indian citizens: visa-free entry up to 30 days (business, tourism, social, transit), extended through 31 Dec 2026. Mandatory: Malaysia Digital Arrival Card (MDAC) submitted within 3 days before arrival.

apply_how: Fill the MDAC online within 3 days before arrival (imigresen-online.imi.gov.my).
Carry onward ticket + accommodation proof.

sources.title: Jabatan Imigresen Malaysia — visa requirement by country

name: Costa Rica

base.summary: Indian passport alone: consular visa required.

us_visa.summary: Visa-exempt with a valid MULTIPLE-ENTRY US (or Canada/EU) visa valid ≥ 6 months from your Costa Rica entry date — stay up to 30 days (extendable to 90 in-country). Passport needs 180 days validity.

green_card.summary: US permanent residence also qualifies (same ≥ 6-month validity rule on the document).

apply_how: Carry the valid US visa/GC used to qualify; airlines check validity strictly.
Otherwise: consular visa via the Costa Rican embassy.

sources.title: Dirección General de Migración y Extranjería — visas

name: Panama

base.summary: Indian passport alone: stamped/authorized visa required.

us_visa.summary: Visa-free entry (Executive Decree 196 of 28 Oct 2024) with a MULTIPLE-ENTRY US visa that has been USED at least once and is valid ≥ 6 months on entry. Passport ≥ 3 months.

green_card.summary: Valid US green card similarly qualifies (≥ 6 months validity).

apply_how: Carry passport + qualifying US visa/GC; expect airline document checks.
Otherwise: authorized visa via a Panamanian consulate.

sources.title: Embassy of India, Panama — visa advisory

name: Bahamas

base.summary: Indian passport alone: Bahamian visa required.

us_visa.summary: CORRECTED: Indians with a valid US (or UK/Canada/Schengen) visa are visa-exempt — up to 90 days. The exemption is document-based; official pages do not condition it on arriving from the US.

green_card.summary: US permanent residents (I-551): visa-exempt, but capped at 30 days per the Bahamian MFA.

apply_how: Carry passport + the valid US visa or physical green card.
US pre-clearance operates in Nassau on the return leg — arrive early.
No qualifying document: apply via the Bahamas eVisa/consulate.

sources.title: Bahamas MFA — who does NOT need a visa

name: Brazil

base.summary: CORRECTED: Brazil's tourist eVisa is only for US/Canada/Australia citizens — Indian TOURISTS need a consular visa (VFS submission). A business e-Visa exists for eligible Indians (DigiLocker/Aadhaar e-sign). Feb 2026 India–Brazil agreement: visitor/business visas now issued with 10-year validity.

apply_how: Tourism: consular visa via VFS Global for Brazil with jurisdiction over your residence.
Business (eligible Indians): e-visa at the official contracted portal brazil.vfsevisa.com, ~3–5 working days, DigiLocker e-signature required.
Ask for the 10-year multiple-entry validity under the 2026 bilateral arrangement.

sources.title: Embassy of India, Brasília — visa pages
